data.content:
The build confirms the file is generated (`obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`) but the compiler doesn't see it. The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes the file but never adds it to the `Compile` item group, so MSBuild doesn't pass it to the compiler.

Fix: add the generated file to `Compile` items in the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. The file is written to disk, but MSBuild doesn't know to include it in compilation.

The fix is to add an `<ItemGroup>` with a `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` inside the target, after generating the file.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

  </Target>", "new_str": "    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>"}

  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds and runs correctly.

**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote the `.cs` file to disk but never added it to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild only compiles files it knows about through `<Compile>` items, so the generated file was silently ignored.

**Fix:** Added `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` inside the target so the generated file is included in compilation.
